examples.title: Pseudocode: Binary Tree Traversals
examples.description: Demonstrates the three depth-first traversal algorithms: Pre-order (NLR), In-order (LNR), and Post-order (LRN)
examples.code:
ALGORITHM PreOrder(node)
// Visit root, then left subtree, then right subtree
INPUT: node - current tree node
OUTPUT: List of visited nodes

BEGIN
 IF node == NULL THEN
 RETURN
 END IF
 
 VISIT(node) // Process root first
 PreOrder(node.left) // Traverse left subtree
 PreOrder(node.right) // Traverse right subtree
END

---

ALGORITHM InOrder(node)
// Visit left subtree, then root, then right subtree
INPUT: node - current tree node
OUTPUT: List of visited nodes

BEGIN
 IF node == NULL THEN
 RETURN
 END IF
 
 InOrder(node.left) // Traverse left subtree
 VISIT(node) // Process root
 InOrder(node.right) // Traverse right subtree
END

---

ALGORITHM PostOrder(node)
// Visit left subtree, then right subtree, then root
INPUT: node - current tree node
OUTPUT: List of visited nodes

BEGIN
 IF node == NULL THEN
 RETURN
 END IF
 
 PostOrder(node.left) // Traverse left subtree
 PostOrder(node.right) // Traverse right subtree
 VISIT(node) // Process root last
END

---

ALGORITHM LevelOrder(node)
// Breadth-first: Visit level by level
INPUT: node - root of tree
OUTPUT: List of visited nodes

BEGIN
 IF node == NULL THEN
 RETURN
 END IF
 
 CREATE queue Q
 ENQUEUE(Q, node)
 
 WHILE Q is not empty DO
 temp = DEQUEUE(Q)
 VISIT(temp)
 
 IF temp.left != NULL THEN
 ENQUEUE(Q, temp.left)
 END IF
 
 IF temp.right != NULL THEN
 ENQUEUE(Q, temp.right)
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: The pseudocode demonstrates four fundamental binary tree traversal algorithms. Pre-order (NLR) visits root first - useful for copying trees. In-order (LNR) produces sorted output for BSTs. Post-order (LRN) visits root last - useful for deleting trees. Level-order uses a queue for breadth-first traversal.

examples.title: Python: Binary Search Tree Implementation
examples.description: Complete BST implementation with node creation, insertion, and search operations
examples.explanation: This Python implementation shows a complete Binary Search Tree with insertion that maintains BST property (left < root < right). The search operation has O(h) time complexity where h is height. In-order traversal of a BST always produces sorted output.
examples.timeComplexity: Search: O(h), Insert: O(h), In-order: O(n)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) for recursion stack, O(n) for traversal result

examples.title: C: Binary Tree Implementation with Traversals
examples.description: Complete C implementation of binary tree node structure and all traversal algorithms
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates the classic pointer-based binary tree structure. Each node contains data and two child pointers. The traversal functions use recursion to visit nodes in different orders. This is the fundamental implementation style taught in computer science courses.
examples.timeComplexity: All traversals: O(n), Height: O(n), Count: O(n)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(h) for recursion stack where h is height, worst case O(n)

examples.title: Python: BST Search and Deletion Operations
examples.description: Binary Search Tree with advanced operations including search, insert, and deletion with all three cases
examples.explanation: This implementation covers all three deletion cases in BST: (1) leaf node - simply remove it, (2) node with one child - replace with that child, (3) node with two children - replace with inorder successor (smallest in right subtree) and delete the successor. This is a critical operation in BSTs.
examples.timeComplexity: Search: O(h), Insert: O(h), Delete: O(h)
